candel
  • API Reference
  • candel
  • View page source

candel

Cosmography

Various cosmography functions for converting between distance indicators.

class candel.cosmography.Distmod2Distance(Om0=0.3, zmin_interp=1e-08, zmax_interp=0.5, npoints_interp=1000, is_scalar=False)[source]

Bases: object

Interpolator to convert distance modulus to comoving distance in Mpc. Choice of h is determined when calling the __call__ method.

Parameters:

  • Om0 (float) – Matter density parameter.

  • zmin_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • zmax_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • npoints_interp (int) – Number of points in the interpolation grid.

  • is_scalar (bool) – If True, the interpolator is not vectorized. This is useful for debugging, but should be set to False for performance.

class candel.cosmography.Distance2Distmod(Om0=0.3, zmin_interp=1e-08, zmax_interp=0.5, npoints_interp=1000, is_scalar=False)[source]

Bases: object

Interpolator to convert distance in Mpc to distance modulus. Choice of h is determined when calling the __call__ method.

Parameters:

  • Om0 (float) – Matter density parameter.

  • zmin_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • zmax_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • npoints_interp (int) – Number of points in the interpolation grid.

  • is_scalar (bool) – If True, the interpolator is not vectorized. This is useful for debugging, but should be set to False for performance.

class candel.cosmography.Distance2LogAngDist(Om0=0.3, zmin_interp=1e-08, zmax_interp=0.5, npoints_interp=1000, is_scalar=False)[source]

Bases: object

Interpolator to convert distance in Mpc to log angular diameter distance. h is assumed to be one.

Parameters:

  • Om0 (float) – Matter density parameter.

  • zmin_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • zmax_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • npoints_interp (int) – Number of points in the interpolation grid.

  • is_scalar (bool) – If True, the interpolator is not vectorized. This is useful for debugging, but should be set to False for performance.

class candel.cosmography.Distance2LogLumDist(Om0=0.3, zmin_interp=1e-08, zmax_interp=0.5, npoints_interp=1000, is_scalar=False)[source]

Bases: object

Interpolator to convert distance in Mpc to log luminosity distance. h is assumed to be one.

Parameters:

  • Om0 (float) – Matter density parameter.

  • zmin_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • zmax_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • npoints_interp (int) – Number of points in the interpolation grid.

  • is_scalar (bool) – If True, the interpolator is not vectorized. This is useful for debugging, but should be set to False for performance.

class candel.cosmography.LogAngularDiameterDistance2Distmod(Om0=0.3, zmin_interp=1e-08, zmax_interp=0.5, npoints_interp=1000)[source]

Bases: object

Interpolator to convert log angular diameter distance in Mpc to distance modulus. Choice of h is determined when calling the __call__ method.

Parameters:

  • Om0 (float) – Matter density parameter.

  • zmin_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • zmax_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • npoints_interp (int) – Number of points in the interpolation grid.

class candel.cosmography.Distmod2Redshift(Om0=0.3, zmin_interp=1e-08, zmax_interp=0.5, npoints_interp=1000)[source]

Bases: object

Interpolator to convert distance modulus to redshift. Choice of h is determined when calling the __call__ method.

Parameters:

  • Om0 (float) – Matter density parameter.

  • zmin_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • zmax_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • npoints_interp (int) – Number of points in the interpolation grid.

class candel.cosmography.Redshift2Distance(Om0=0.3, zmin_interp=1e-08, zmax_interp=0.5, npoints_interp=1000, is_scalar=False)[source]

Bases: object

Interpolator to convert redshift to comoving distance in Mpc. Choice of h is determined when calling the __call__ method.

Parameters:

  • Om0 (float) – Matter density parameter.

  • zmin_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • zmax_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • npoints_interp (int) – Number of points in the interpolation grid.

  • is_scalar (bool) – If True, the interpolator is not vectorized. This is useful for debugging, but should be set to False for performance.

class candel.cosmography.Redshift2Distmod(Om0=0.3, zmin_interp=1e-08, zmax_interp=0.5, npoints_interp=1000)[source]

Bases: object

Interpolator to convert redshift to distance modulus. Choice of h is determined when calling the __call__ method.

Parameters:

  • Om0 (float) – Matter density parameter.

  • zmin_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • zmax_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • npoints_interp (int) – Number of points in the interpolation grid.

class candel.cosmography.Distance2Redshift(Om0=0.3, zmin_interp=1e-08, zmax_interp=0.5, npoints_interp=1000)[source]

Bases: object

Interpolator to convert comoving distance in Mpc to redshift. Choice of h is determined when calling the __call__ method.

Parameters:

  • Om0 (float) – Matter density parameter.

  • zmin_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • zmax_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • npoints_interp (int) – Number of points in the interpolation grid.

class candel.cosmography.LogGrad_Distmod2ComovingDistance(Om0=0.3, zmin_interp=1e-08, zmax_interp=0.5, npoints_interp=1000)[source]

Bases: object

Interpolator to compute the log gradient of the comoving distance in Mpc / h with respect to distance modulus. Choice of h is determined when calling the __call__ method.

The function is: log (dr / dmu) | mu.

Parameters:

  • Om0 (float) – Matter density parameter.

  • zmin_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • zmax_interp (float) – Minimum and maximum redshift for the interpolation grid.

  • npoints_interp (int) – Number of points in the interpolation grid.

class candel.cosmography.Distance2Distmod_withOm(rmin=0.001, rmax=500, nr=500, Om_min=0.01, Om_max=0.99, nOm=500, zmin_outer=1e-09, zmax_outer=0.3, method='cubic')[source]

Bases: object

Interpolator to convert distance in Mpc to distance modulus, as a function of h and Om, which are specified on the fly.

class candel.cosmography.Distance2Redshift_withOm(rmin=0.001, rmax=500, nr=500, Om_min=0.01, Om_max=0.99, nOm=500, zmin_outer=1e-09, zmax_outer=0.3, method='cubic')[source]

Bases: object

Interpolator to convert distance in Mpc to redshift, as a function of h and Om, which are specified on the fly.

candel.cosmography.redshift_to_dL_cosmography(z, H0, q0=-0.55, j0=1, s0=0.055)[source]

Calculate the luminosity distance for a given redshift using cosmographic expansion up to second order in redshift.

Inference

Running the MCMC inference for the model and some postprocessing.

candel.inference.find_initial_point(model, model_kwargs, maxiter=100, seed=42)[source]

Run L-BFGS to find a reasonable MCMC starting point.

Traces the model from the prior, maps to unconstrained space, runs scipy L-BFGS-B with JAX autodiff gradients, then maps back to constrained space. Returns None if the optimisation fails.

candel.inference.print_evidence(bic, aic, lnZ_laplace, err_lnZ_laplace, lnZ_harmonic, err_lnZ_harmonic)[source]
candel.inference.run_pv_inference(model, model_kwargs, print_summary=True, save_samples=True, return_original_samples=False, init_maxiter=None)[source]

Run MCMC inference on the given PV model, post-process the samples, optionally compute the BIC, AIC, evidence and save the samples to an HDF5 file.

If init_maxiter > 0, runs L-BFGS to find a good starting point before launching NUTS.

candel.inference.run_H0_inference(model, model_kwargs=None, print_summary=True, save_samples=True)[source]

Run MCMC inference on an H0 model, post-process the samples, plot the corner plot and optionally save the samples to an HDF5 file.

candel.inference.get_log_density(samples, model, model_kwargs, batch_size=5)[source]

Compute the log density of NumPyro model. The batch size cannot be much larger to prevent exhausting the memory.

candel.inference.extract_auxiliary(samples, keys)[source]

Extract and remove auxiliary deterministic samples.

Parameters:

  • samples (dict) – Dictionary returned by mcmc.get_samples().

  • keys (sequence of str) – Names to extract. If a key ends with _skipZ, the suffix is dropped in the returned dictionary.

candel.inference.drop_deterministic(samples, check_all_equals=True)[source]

Drop deterministic and latent variable samples.

candel.inference.postprocess_samples(samples)[source]

Postprocess MCMC samples.

candel.inference.print_clean_summary(samples)[source]

Wrapper around numpyro’s print_summary.

candel.inference.save_mcmc_samples(samples, log_density, log_density_per_sample, gof, filename, auxiliary=None)[source]

Save the MCMC samples to an HDF5 file.

Evidence

Evidence and BIC/AIC calculation for MCMC samples.

candel.evidence.BIC_AIC(samples, log_density, ndata)[source]

Get the BIC/AIC of HMC samples from a Numpyro model.

Parameters:

  • samples (dict) – Dictionary of samples from the Numpyro MCMC object.

  • log_density (numpy array) – Log density of the samples.

  • ndata (int) – Number of data points.

Returns:

BIC, AIC

Return type:

floats

candel.evidence.dict_samples_to_array(samples)[source]

Convert a dictionary of samples to a 2-dimensional array.

candel.evidence.harmonic_evidence(samples_arr, log_density, temperature=0.8, epochs_num=20, return_flow_samples=True, verbose=True)[source]

Calculate the evidence using the harmonic package. The model has a few more hyperparameters that are set to defaults now.

Parameters:

  • samples_arr (3-dimensional array) – MCMC samples of shape (nchains, nsamples, ndim).

  • log_density (2-dimensional array) – Log posterior xs of shape (nchains, nsamples).

  • temperature (float, optional) – Temperature of the harmonic model.

  • epochs_num (int, optional) – Number of epochs for training the model.

  • return_flow_samples (bool, optional) – Whether to return the flow samples.

  • verbose (bool, optional) – Whether to print progress.

Returns:

  • ln_evidence, err_ln_inv_evidence (float and tuple of floats) – The log evidence and its error.

  • flow_samples (2-dimensional array, optional) – Flow samples of shape (nsamples, ndim). To check their agreement with the input samples.

candel.evidence.laplace_evidence(samples_array, log_density)[source]

Calculate the evidence using the Laplace approximation. Calculates the mean and error of the log evidence estimated from the chains.

Parameters:

  • samples_array (3-dimensional array) – MCMC samples of shape (nchains, nsamples, ndim).

  • log_density (2-dimensional array) – Log posterior xs of shape (nchains, nsamples).

Returns:

mean_ln_inv_evidence, err_ln_inv_evidence

Return type:

two floats

Utilities

Various utility functions for candel.

candel.util.fsection(title, width=60)[source]

Print a section header.

candel.util.fprint(*args, verbose=True, **kwargs)[source]

Print an indented status message.

candel.util.convert_none_strings(d)[source]

Convert all string values in a dictionary to None if they are equal to “none” (case insensitive). This is useful for parsing TOML files where “none” is used to represent None values.

candel.util.replace_prior_with_delta(config, param, value, verbose=True)[source]

Replace the prior of param with a delta distribution at value.

candel.util.convert_to_absolute_paths(config)[source]

Recursively convert relative paths in config to absolute paths.

candel.util.load_config(config_path, replace_none=True, fill_paths=True, replace_los_prior=True)[source]

Load a TOML configuration file and convert “none” strings to None.

candel.util.get_nested(config, key_path, default=None)[source]

Recursively access a nested value using a slash-separated key.

candel.util.radec_to_cartesian(ra, dec)[source]

Convert right ascension and declination (in degrees) to unit Cartesian coordinates.

candel.util.cartesian_to_radec(x, y, z)[source]

Convert Cartesian coordinates (x, y, z) to right ascension and declination (RA, Dec), both in degrees.

candel.util.radec_to_galactic(ra, dec)[source]

Convert right ascension and declination to galactic coordinates (all in degrees).

candel.util.galactic_to_radec(ell, b)[source]

Convert galactic coordinates to right ascension and declination (all in degrees).

candel.util.galactic_to_radec_cartesian(ell, b)[source]

Convert galactic coordinates (ell, b) in degrees to ICRS Cartesian unit vectors.

candel.util.query_ned_cmb_redshift_radec(name)[source]

Query NED for heliocentric redshift and ICRS coordinates and return the CMB-frame velocity (cz_CMB) derived from the NED redshift.

candel.util.supergalactic_to_radec(sgl, sgb)[source]

Convert supergalactic coordinates (sgl, sgb) to equatorial right ascension and declination (RA, Dec), all in degrees.

candel.util.radec_to_supergalactic(ra, dec)[source]

Convert right ascension and declination (in degrees) to supergalactic coordinates in degrees.

candel.util.radec_cartesian_to_galactic(x, y, z)[source]

Convert ICRS Cartesian vectors (x, y, z) to Galactic coordinates (ell, b) in degrees, and return the vector magnitude.

candel.util.hms_to_degrees(hours, minutes=None, seconds=None)[source]

Convert hours, minutes and seconds to degrees.

candel.util.dms_to_degrees(degrees, arcminutes=None, arcseconds=None)[source]

Convert degrees, arcminutes and arcseconds to decimal degrees.

candel.util.heliocentric_to_cmb(z_helio, RA, dec, e_z_helio=None)[source]

Convert heliocentric redshift to CMB redshift using the Planck 2018 CMB dipole.

candel.util.name2label(name)[source]

Map internal parameter names to LaTeX labels, optionally including catalogue prefix.

candel.util.name2labelgetdist(name)[source]

Return a GetDist-compatible LaTeX label (no $…$) for a parameter, optionally prepending the catalogue name as plain text.

Example:

“CF4_W1/beta” → r”mathrm{CF4~W1}, beta”

candel.util.sort_params(keys)[source]
candel.util.plot_corner(samples, show_fig=True, filename=None, smooth=1, keys=None)[source]

Plot a corner plot from posterior samples.

candel.util.plot_Vext_rad_corner(samples, show_fig=True, filename=None, smooth=1)[source]

Plot a corner plot of Vext_rad_{mag, ell, b} samples.

candel.util.plot_corner_getdist(samples_list, labels=None, cols=None, show_fig=True, filename=None, keys=None, fontsize=None, legend_fontsize=None, filled=True, apply_ell_offset=False, mag_range=None, ell_range=None, b_range=None, points=None, ranges=None, truths=None)[source]

Plot a GetDist triangle plot for one or more posterior samples.

candel.util.plot_corner_from_hdf5(fnames, keys=None, labels=None, cols=None, fontsize=None, legend_fontsize=None, filled=True, show_fig=True, filename=None, apply_ell_offset=False, mag_range=None, ell_range=None, b_range=None, points=None, ranges=None, truths=None)[source]

Plot a triangle plot from one or more HDF5 files containing posterior samples.

candel.util.interpolate_scalar_field(V, r, rbins, method='cubic')[source]

Interpolate scalar field along last axis with constant extrapolation.

candel.util.interpolate_latitude_field(b_deg, r, rbins, method='cubic')[source]

Interpolate latitude via sin(b); return degrees.

candel.util.interpolate_longitude_field(l_deg, r, rbins, method='cubic')[source]

Interpolate longitude via sin/cos; return degrees in [0, 360).

candel.util.interpolate_all_radial_fields(model, Vmag, ell, b, r_eval_size=1000, method='cubic')[source]
candel.util.get_percentiles_circ_along_r(lon_deg_samples, qs=(2.5, 16, 50, 84, 97.5), rmin_conc=0.2, return_unwrapped=True)[source]

Robust circular percentiles along radius with phase-tracked reference.

candel.util.deg_wrap_360(y, pos)[source]
candel.util.plot_radial_profiles(samples, model, r_eval_size=1000, show_fig=True, filename=None)[source]

Plot the radial profiles of Vext_rad_{mag, ell, b} from the samples, including 1sigma and 2sigma percentile bands.

candel.util.plot_Vext_radmag(samples, model, r_eval_size=1000, show_fig=True, filename=None)[source]
candel.util.plot_Vext_moll(samples_pix, fname_out, coord_in='C', coord_out='G', lon_step=60, lat_step=30, eps=1e-12, nside_plot=None, remove_coord_label=True)[source]
Plot three stacked Mollweide maps from MCMC samples (Nsamples, Npix):

row 1: mean row 2: std row 3: mean/std

If nside_plot > nside(map), upsample via healpy bilinear interpolation.

candel.util.read_gof(fname, which, raise_error=True)[source]

Read goodness-of-fit statistics from a file with samples.

candel.util.read_samples(root, fname, keys=None)[source]
candel.util.get_dlog_density_stats(lpA, lpB)[source]

Compute the mean and standard deviation of the difference in log density between two sets of log densities of shape (num_samples, num_objects).
